meaning_english: To base your hopes on something / to rely on. Synonyms: Rely on, Depend on, Count on.
To finally stop opposing something / to collapse inward. Synonyms: Yield, Surrender, Buckle.
To take strict action to prevent or stop something. Synonyms: Suppress, Restrict, Crack down.
To begin to understand something (often slowly). Synonyms: Comprehend, Grasp, Realize.
To think or speak about something for a long time (usually something negative). Synonyms: Elaborate, Harp on.
To use up a large part of something (like savings or time). Synonyms: Consume, Eradicate, Corrode.
To agree with a plan or to start associating with people (often bad). Synonyms: Concur, Associate.
To worry about something unnecessarily. Synonyms: Worry, Brood, Agonize.
To ignore or avoid dealing with a difficult fact or mistake. Synonyms: Mask, Hide, Conceal.
To reach an agreement after long or difficult discussion. Synonyms: Negotiate, Finalize, Settle.
When the result of something depends entirely on one factor. Synonyms: Depend on, Pivot on.
To stay away from a place or to avoid a particular food/topic. Synonyms: Avoid, Abstain, Evade.
To reveal a secret or information. Synonyms: Reveal, Disclose, Divulge.
To watch something happen without taking part. Synonyms: Observe, Witness, Spectate.
To move towards a place or to result in something. Synonyms: Head for, Contribute to.
To be good enough or to reach the required standard. Synonyms: Match, Qualify, Equal.
To think about something carefully for a period of time. Synonyms: Ponder, Ruminate, Contemplate.
To settle a quarrel or to fix something temporarily. Synonyms: Reconcile, Repair, Mend.
To try to make something seem less important than it is. Synonyms: Understate, Minimize, Belittle.
To survive a dangerous illness or a very difficult situation. Synonyms: Recover, Survive.
To accumulate or achieve a large number of something (points, debt). Synonyms: Accumulate, Amass.
To mention something unpleasant from the past that was forgotten. Synonyms: Revive, Recall.
To start doing something with determination. Synonyms: Begin, Commence, Undertake.
To support or strengthen something that is weak or failing. Synonyms: Bolster, Reinforce, Support.
To increase the speed/intensity or to take responsibility. Synonyms: Increase, Intensify.
To speak to someone as if they are less intelligent or important. Synonyms: Patronize, Condescend.
To speak angrily to someone because they have done wrong. Synonyms: Scold, Reprimand, Rebuke.
To be consistent or connected with something else. Synonyms: Match, Accord, Coincide.
To mention a subject briefly during a speech or writing. Synonyms: Mention, Allude to.
To cancel a debt or to dismiss someone as a failure. Synonyms: Cancel, Disregard, Ignore.
